sides.label: Acquirer
sides.description: Buy-side principal organisation. Deal lead, integration lead, HR/People lead, board observer, deal partner.
sides.overrideNotes: Acquirer answers about the target are observer-evidence, not self-assessment.

sides.label: Target
sides.description: Sell-side principal organisation. CEO/founder, senior leadership, key talent.
sides.overrideNotes: Target answers about own organisation are self-assessment. See sheet 16 for override rules.

sides.label: Advisor
sides.description: External professional advisor: investment bank, legal counsel, consulting firm, accountant.
sides.overrideNotes: Advisor evidence weighted as second-hand unless documented in working files.

sides.label: Board
sides.description: Board observer, non-executive director, operating partner with board mandate.
sides.overrideNotes: Board evidence treated as direct observation only when documented in board materials.

sides.label: External
sides.description: External party with structural visibility: prior acquirer, departed senior employee, regulator, key customer.
sides.overrideNotes: External evidence requires documentary corroboration to exceed confidence: low.

sides.label: Other
sides.description: Any role not covered above. Free-text role description required.
sides.overrideNotes: Default treatment: confidence: low until role-specific weighting is set.

roles.legitimateAccessScope: Strategic rationale, valuation, deal structure, target leadership relationship at owner level
Day-1, Day-100, operating-model design, governance migration, decision rights
Retention package design, role-mapping, performance system migration, exit planning
Revenue synergy, customer migration, market overlap, pricing harmonisation
Financial diligence, working capital, accounting policy migration, control environment
Reps and warranties, employment contracts, regulatory clearance, governance materials
Board-level rationale, strategic governance, post-close oversight design
Strategic intent, owner-level commitments, autonomy negotiation, key-person dependencies
Functional operating reality, political dynamics, team-level continuity
Tacit operational knowledge, customer/IP dependencies, replacement difficulty
Compensation history, performance system in use, retention risks, cultural narrative
Financial controls, accounting practice, governance compliance, reporting cadence
Process design, market read, competing-bidder dynamics, pricing context
Documentary trail, dispute history, structural risk areas, regulatory exposure
Specific workstream evidence: commercial, operational, technology, HR diligence
Board-level visibility into governance, strategic decisions, leadership performance
Sponsor-level pattern recognition across portfolio, integration playbook authority
Historical operating reality, departure mechanism, retained-employee comparison
Prior diligence findings, deal-process behaviour, governance precedent
Service-level reality, dependency dynamics, change-of-control behaviour
Requires free-text role description and analyst review for access scoping

evidenceTypes.label: Direct Observation
evidenceTypes.definition: Respondent personally observed the event in question, in their own role, in the relevant timeframe.

evidenceTypes.label: Document-Supported
evidenceTypes.definition: Respondent's answer is corroborated by a specific named document referenced in the EvidenceItem table.

evidenceTypes.label: Reported by Others
evidenceTypes.definition: Respondent learned the information from a colleague, subordinate, or peer rather than observing it directly.

evidenceTypes.label: Inference
evidenceTypes.definition: Respondent did not observe the event but is reasoning from related facts they did observe.

evidenceTypes.label: Hypothetical
evidenceTypes.definition: No actual case exists. Respondent is describing what they expect would happen based on perceived organisational pattern.

evidenceTypes.label: Unknown / Cannot Answer
evidenceTypes.definition: Respondent explicitly cannot answer from any basis. Must NOT be coerced into a substantive choice.

evidenceTypes.code: Hypothetical answers and unknown answers are NEVER aggregated into the primary environment score. They are stored for divergence-detection and missing-evidence analysis only.

Document-supported answers carry the same nominal weight as direct observation, but only if the EvidenceItem is reviewed by an analyst and not contradicted by a more recent or higher-authority document.

knowledgeLevels.label: First-Hand
knowledgeLevels.definition: Respondent was a participant or direct decision-maker in the event.

knowledgeLevels.label: Second-Hand
knowledgeLevels.definition: Respondent was present in the same organisational context but did not participate in the specific decision.

knowledgeLevels.label: Document-Based
knowledgeLevels.definition: Respondent did not witness the event but has access to authoritative documentation of it.

knowledgeLevels.label: Pattern-Based
knowledgeLevels.definition: Respondent generalises from repeated similar events rather than describing a specific instance.

knowledgeLevels.label: Speculative
knowledgeLevels.definition: Respondent reasons about what might have happened or might happen.

knowledgeLevels.label: Not Known
knowledgeLevels.definition: Respondent has no basis for the answer. Use only with EvidenceType: unknown.

confidenceLevels.label: High
confidenceLevels.definition: Respondent is confident the answer accurately reflects the underlying reality.

confidenceLevels.label: Medium
confidenceLevels.definition: Respondent is reasonably confident but acknowledges some uncertainty.

confidenceLevels.label: Low
confidenceLevels.definition: Respondent is uncertain. Answer is best available but not strongly held.

confidenceLevels.label: Cannot Determine
confidenceLevels.definition: Respondent has insufficient basis to assign confidence. Used with EvidenceType: unknown.

reliabilityFlags.label: Contradicted by another respondent
reliabilityFlags.triggerCondition: A different respondent on the same side answered the same question with a materially different observation.
reliabilityFlags.effectOnScoring: Both answers downweighted by 0.5 until analyst resolves.

reliabilityFlags.label: Contradicted by document
reliabilityFlags.triggerCondition: A reviewed EvidenceItem materially contradicts the answer.
reliabilityFlags.effectOnScoring: Answer downweighted to 0.20; document evidence carries forward.

reliabilityFlags.label: Socially desirable response
reliabilityFlags.triggerCondition: Answer matches the most flattering organisational self-description for that question.
reliabilityFlags.effectOnScoring: Answer downweighted by 0.7; flagged for analyst review.

reliabilityFlags.label: Evasive / non-answer
reliabilityFlags.triggerCondition: Respondent provided a meta-comment, scope objection, or generality without engaging the substantive question.
reliabilityFlags.effectOnScoring: Answer scored as unknown; flagged for analyst review.

reliabilityFlags.label: Overgeneralised
reliabilityFlags.triggerCondition: Respondent answered for the entire organisation when their access scope is narrower.
reliabilityFlags.effectOnScoring: Answer downweighted by 0.5; flagged for role-mismatch review.

reliabilityFlags.label: Speaks for group without access
reliabilityFlags.triggerCondition: Respondent answered a question outside their legitimate access map (Sheet 2_Roles).
reliabilityFlags.effectOnScoring: Answer scored as inference at most, regardless of self-reported EvidenceType.

reliabilityFlags.label: Hypothetical answer
reliabilityFlags.triggerCondition: No actual case exists; answer is about expected behaviour.
reliabilityFlags.effectOnScoring: Answer excluded from primary score; retained for divergence and missing-evidence analysis.

reliabilityFlags.label: Structurally unlikely event
reliabilityFlags.triggerCondition: Answer describes an event that the surrounding organisational structure makes formally improbable (e.g. senior accountability event in deflection-heavy environment).
reliabilityFlags.effectOnScoring: Answer flagged for analyst pattern-recognition review.

reliabilityFlags.label: No direct knowledge
reliabilityFlags.triggerCondition: Respondent explicitly declared no direct observation or document basis.
reliabilityFlags.effectOnScoring: Answer excluded from primary score; preserved for coverage map.

answer.description: Globally unique identifier for this answer record.
Foreign key to the Deal record this answer belongs to.
Stable identifier of the question, e.g. ACQ-Q1, TGT-OBS-Q5.
Foreign key to the Respondent record.
Multiple-choice selection. Null permitted only when freeText is present.
Open-text response. Required when no option is selected.
Has the respondent personally observed this in the relevant timeframe? Set BEFORE seeing options.
Respondent proximity to the underlying event.
Zero or more reliability concerns. May be respondent-set, engine-set, or analyst-set.
When the question is about specific roles (e.g. "leadership team"), list which roles the respondent actually observed.
List of EvidenceItem references that corroborate this answer.
Set if the answer was revised (e.g. respondent returned to amend).
Set by analyst during review. Tracks whether the analyst has applied special treatment to this answer.
Analyst free-text note attached to this answer.

answer.field: Validation rule (engine-enforced): if directObservationGate = no, evidenceType must be one of {reported_by_others, inference, hypothetical, unknown}. directObservationGate = yes does NOT permit unknown.

Validation rule (engine-enforced): if evidenceType = unknown, confidence must be cannot_determine, knowledgeLevel must be not_known, and selectedOption must be E ("I cannot answer from direct observation").

respondent.description: Deal this respondent is participating in.

respondent.type: enum: c_suite / vp / director / manager / ic / external

respondent.description: How long the respondent has occupied their current role. < 18 months downweighted per ST methodology.
How this respondent engaged with the diagnostic.

respondent.type: enum: none / professional / personal / family / financial

respondent.description: Has the analyst reviewed and verified this respondent's role and access scope.

evidenceItem.type: enum: document / interview / dataroom_extract / public_record / other

evidenceItem.description: Date the item was produced. Null if unknown.
Which questionnaire questions this item bears on.
Which risk categories this item bears on.
URI or dataroom path where the item is stored. Sensitive: encrypted at rest.
Analyst-curated summary of the evidentially relevant content.

analystAssessment.description: Which risk category this assessment addresses.
Evidence items that support the finding.

analystAssessment.type: enum: directly_observed / document_supported / inferred / unknown / contradicted / follow_up_required
analystAssessment.description: Per the brief's section 16: every finding must label the evidentiary basis.

analystAssessment.type: enum: critical / high / medium / low / informational

analystAssessment.description: Senior analyst who approved (required if approvalStatus = approved).
Set when approvalStatus moves to approved.

contradiction.type: enum: cross_respondent_same_side / cross_side / answer_vs_document / answer_vs_pattern / self_contradiction

contradiction.description: Documents in tension with the answers.

contradiction.type: enum: critical / high / medium / low / informational

contradiction.description: If resolution = escalated_to_finding, points to the AnalystAssessment that contains the finding.
Set when reviewStatus moves to resolved or escalated.

riskOutput.description: See sheet 13_RiskCategories for the canonical list.

riskOutput.type: enum: critical / high / medium / low / informational
riskOutput.description: Composite severity for this risk category.
Composite confidence — capped by sheet 15_ConfidenceGate rules.
Coverage limitations: which roles answered, which evidence types dominate.
Aggregated pre-close recommendations from constituent assessments.

riskOutput.type: enum: very_high / high / medium / low / very_low / cannot_determine
riskOutput.description: Per brief section 13: dedicated 6–18 month failure-probability output line.
Which final-report section this risk output appears in. See ST_B_Single_Output_Template_v1 (Phase 3 rebuild).

riskCategories.label: Integration Fracture Risk
riskCategories.definition: Probability the integration produces a structural break in the combined organisation: leadership exits, parallel operations, governance impasse.
riskCategories.primarySignalSources: Acquirer authority mechanism + Target acquiescence pattern + Cross-side divergence.

riskCategories.label: Leadership Accountability Risk
riskCategories.definition: Probability senior actors evade accountability post-close, creating misalignment between formal commitments and observed behaviour.
riskCategories.primarySignalSources: Accountability symmetry questions + observed responsibility-shedding pattern.

riskCategories.label: Key-Person Retention Risk
riskCategories.definition: Probability that pivotal target operators exit within the 18-month window post-close.
riskCategories.primarySignalSources: Retention mechanism questions + departure-mechanism precedents + compensation lock-in evidence.

riskCategories.label: Founder Dependency Risk
riskCategories.definition: Probability that target operating performance is contingent on founder presence.
riskCategories.primarySignalSources: Decision-rights pattern + key-person dependency mapping + pre-close founder commitments.

riskCategories.label: Decision-Rights Conflict Risk
riskCategories.definition: Probability of post-close conflict over who decides what.
riskCategories.primarySignalSources: Authority mechanism + integration-planning response patterns + governance documentation.

riskCategories.label: Governance Risk
riskCategories.definition: Probability of governance failure: control environment gaps, board misalignment, regulatory exposure.
riskCategories.primarySignalSources: Documentary evidence + finance/legal respondent answers + governance materials.

riskCategories.label: Political Protection Risk
riskCategories.definition: Probability that internal coalitions protect specific actors at the cost of operational performance.
riskCategories.primarySignalSources: Advancement-pattern analysis + observed exit mechanisms + cross-respondent divergence on accountability.

riskCategories.label: Talent Flight Risk
riskCategories.definition: Probability of broad-based talent departure beyond key-person retention concerns.
riskCategories.primarySignalSources: Resource-flow questions + cultural-narrative comparison + people-function answers.

riskCategories.label: Management Performance Drop Risk
riskCategories.definition: Probability that management effectiveness materially declines post-close.
riskCategories.primarySignalSources: Strategy-decision questions + commercial-period response patterns + integration-planning behaviour.

riskCategories.label: Months 6–18 Failure Risk
riskCategories.definition: Composite forward-looking failure indicator for the integration's second half-year through 18-month mark.
riskCategories.primarySignalSources: All of the above, weighted by analyst severity assignment.

roleCoverageMatrix.tier: Coverage gap behaviour: if a Tier-mandatory role is not represented in the respondent set, the engine flags coverage_insufficient and confidence is capped at medium for any risk category that depends on that role.

confidenceGate.notes: Computed across the deal's full answer set.
Triangulation = at least 2 respondents on the same question with materially aligned answers.

confidenceGate.threshold: Documentary corroboration per critical risk category
confidenceGate.notes: Critical = severity ≥ high in any constituent assessment.
Computed across the deal's full answer set.

confidenceGate.requiredLevelForMedium: Tier minimum met for at least one side

overridePrecedence.scenario: Target self-assessment diverges from acquirer observer
overridePrecedence.defaultTreatment: Acquirer-observer answer enters scoring. Self-assessment retained as divergence record.
overridePrecedence.notes: Self-assessment NEVER overrides observer evidence in canonical scoring.

overridePrecedence.scenario: Target self-assessment diverges from documentary evidence
overridePrecedence.defaultTreatment: Documentary evidence governs. Self-assessment downweighted by socially_desirable flag.

overridePrecedence.scenario: Acquirer observer diverges from documentary evidence
overridePrecedence.defaultTreatment: Documentary evidence governs. Observer answer downweighted by contradicted_by_document flag.

overridePrecedence.scenario: Both sides diverge from each other and from document
overridePrecedence.defaultTreatment: Three-way contradiction. Escalated to analyst as a critical contradiction.

overridePrecedence.scenario: Rationale: target self-assessment is by definition status-protective. Observer evidence has lower reputational stake in target's narrative. This default may be overridden per-deal if the analyst documents specific reasons (e.g. observer is structurally biased).

analystGate.permittedOutputLanguage: "Directional pattern only. Not diligence." Must NOT name a final environment or risk severity.

analystGate.analystReviewRequirement: Analyst review MANDATORY before final report issuance.
analystGate.permittedOutputLanguage: Full risk-category outputs, severity, confidence, pre-close and post-close actions.

analystGate.outputType: Sealed prediction (e.g. Eli Lilly / Ventyx forward-verification)
analystGate.analystReviewRequirement: Analyst review MANDATORY. Senior analyst sign-off required.
analystGate.permittedOutputLanguage: Sealed ECS prediction with locked timestamp and sign-off audit trail.

analystGate.analystReviewRequirement: No analyst review for output, but practitioner certification required.
analystGate.permittedOutputLanguage: Per practitioner training scope. Not a paid report.

analystGate.outputType: No paid output may be issued by automated aggregation alone. The architecture forbids the prior practice of computing an environment pair from summed signals and presenting that as the deliverable.
